text: Say, ňĻO Prophet,ňļ ‚ÄúIt has been revealed to me that a group of jinn listened ňĻto the Quran,ňļ and said ňĻto their fellow jinnňļ: ‚ÄėIndeed, we have heard a wondrous recitation.
It leads to Right Guidance so we believed in it, and we will never associate anyone with our Lord ňĻin worshipňļ.
ňĻNow, we believe thatňļ our Lord‚ÄĒExalted is His Majesty‚ÄĒhas neither taken a mate nor offspring,
and that the foolish of us used to utter ňĻoutrageousňļ falsehoods about Allah. <sup foot_note=1243>1243</sup>

foot_notes.1243: Meaning, evil jinn tempted some pagan Arabs to believe that the angels are Allah‚Äôs daughters through female jinn.

text: We certainly thought that humans and jinn would never speak lies about Allah.
And some men used to seek refuge with some jinn‚ÄĒso they increased each other in wickedness. <sup foot_note=1244>1244</sup>
And those ňĻhumansňļ thought, just like you ňĻjinnňļ, that Allah would not resurrect anyone ňĻfor judgmentňļ.
ňĻEarlierňļ we tried to reach heaven ňĻfor newsňļ, only to find it filled with stern guards and shooting stars. <sup foot_note=1245>1245</sup>

foot_notes.1245: Some jinn used to eavesdrop on the heaven, then pass on what they heard to fortune-tellers. But this practice came to an end once MuŠł•ammad ( Ô∑ļ ) was sent as a messenger with the Quran.

text: We used to take up positions there for eavesdropping, but whoever dares eavesdrop now will find a flare lying in wait for them.
Now, we have no clue whether evil is intended for those on earth, or their Lord intends for them what is right.
Among us are those who are righteous and those who are less so. We have been of different factions.
ňĻNow,ňļ we truly know that we cannot frustrate Allah on earth, nor can we escape from Him ňĻinto heavenňļ.
When we heard the guidance ňĻof the Quranňļ, we ňĻreadilyňļ believed in it. For whoever believes in their Lord will have no fear of being denied ňĻa rewardňļ or wronged.
And among us are those who have submitted ňĻto Allahňļ and those who are deviant. So ňĻas forňļ those who submitted, it is they who have attained Right Guidance.
And as for the deviant, they will be fuel for Hell.‚Äô‚ÄĚ
Had the deniers followed the Right Way, We would have certainly granted them abundant rain to drink‚ÄĒ
as a test for them. <sup foot_note=1246>1246</sup> And whoever turns away from the remembrance of their Lord will be admitted by Him into an overwhelming punishment.

foot_notes.1246: Meaning, to see if they will be grateful to Allah for rain and other provisions.

text: The places of worship are ňĻonlyňļ for Allah, so do not invoke anyone besides Him.
Yet when the servant of Allah <sup foot_note=1247>1247</sup> stood up calling upon Him ňĻaloneňļ, the pagans almost swarmed over him.
Say, ňĻO Prophet,ňļ ‚ÄúI call only upon my Lord, associating none with Him ňĻin worshipňļ.‚ÄĚ
Say, ‚ÄúIt is not in my power to harm or benefit you.‚ÄĚ
Say, ‚ÄúNo one can protect me from Allah ňĻif I were to disobey Himňļ, nor can I find any refuge other than Him.

ňĻMy duty isňļ only to convey ňĻthe truthňļ from Allah and ňĻdeliverňļ His messages.‚ÄĚ And whoever disobeys Allah and His Messenger will certainly be in the Fire of Hell, to stay there for ever and ever.
Only when they see what they have been threatened with will they know who is weaker in helpers and inferior in manpower.
Say, ‚ÄúI do not know if what you are promised is near or my Lord has set a distant time for it.
ňĻHe is theňļ Knower of the unseen, disclosing none of it to anyone,
except messengers of His choice. Then He appoints angel-guards before and behind them
to ensure that the messengers fully deliver the messages of their Lord‚ÄĒthough He ňĻalreadyňļ knows all about them, and keeps account of everything.‚ÄĚ
